channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a wide range of third-party tool integrations, enhancing the capabilities of AI agents for real-world applications. This development allows developers to connect their agents to platforms like GitHub and Notion.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's role in enhancing event experiences. This annual tradition builds excitement for the upcoming tech conference.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas emphasizes in-person networking and hands-on learning, focusing on agentic AI and technical advancements. The event promises specialized tracks for developers to enhance their skills.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, featuring improved GPU acceleration and better support for PyTorch and JAX. This update aims to enhance efficiency for deploying GenAI and advanced on-device inference.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Wednesday Build Hour is a new interactive session by Google Cloud aimed at helping developers improve their cloud skills through hands-on building. The program covers advanced topics like AI agents and productivity tools.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: Gemini Code Assist has introduced updates to streamline coding workflows, including features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These tools aim to enhance collaboration and adapt to individual developer styles.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: New features in Gemini Code Assist for IntelliJ and VS Code, including Finish Changes and Outlines, aim to reduce developer friction. These tools help automate coding tasks and improve code comprehension.

item.title: Dutch healthcare software vendor goes dark after ransomware attack
item.description: A Dutch healthcare software vendor, ChipSoft, has gone offline following a ransomware attack, although email services remain operational. The incident highlights ongoing cybersecurity threats in the healthcare sector.

item.title: Investors are going nuclear to keep UK's AI datacenters fed
item.description: Investors are increasingly funding nuclear and fusion startups in the UK to meet the rising energy demands of AI datacenters. This trend reflects a strategic shift towards sustainable energy solutions.

item.title: I found a 'DISM' command that reclaims Windows 11 system storage - but you'll have to use it wisely
item.description: A new 'DISM' command can help reclaim storage on Windows 11 by managing Reserved Storage, which retains space for updates. Users are advised to use this command wisely to optimize system performance.

item.title: Why you shouldn't buy cheap DisplayPort cables - the 'Death Pin' can put your GPU at serious risk
item.description: Cheap DisplayPort cables can pose serious risks to your GPU due to a manufacturing flaw known as the 'Death Pin'. Users are advised to invest in higher-quality cables to avoid potential damage.

item.title: DXC lands Metropolitan Police outsourcing deal that could climb to £1B
item.description: DXC Technology has secured a contract with the Metropolitan Police worth up to £1 billion to manage business process outsourcing and transition to a new Oracle ERP system. This deal highlights the increasing reliance on technology in public sector operations.

item.title: NHS Scotland-linked domains caught serving pr0n and dodgy sports streams
item.description: Several domains linked to NHS Scotland have been compromised, redirecting users to adult content and illegal sports streams. This breach raises concerns about cybersecurity in healthcare.

item.title: Virtual SG-41 project brings Nazi cipher machine to life in the browser
item.description: A 3D digital model of the SG-41 cipher machine, a potential successor to the Enigma, has been created for online exploration. This project showcases the intersection of history and technology.

item.title: The best Windows laptops of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: A review of the best Windows laptops of 2026 has been published, highlighting performance, portability, and value. This guide helps consumers make informed purchasing decisions.

item.title: UK's grand plan to fuel AI with public data faces uphill battle
item.description: The UK's initiative to create a National Data Library for AI development faces challenges, as officials need to improve data accessibility. Without better datasets, the project may fail to meet its goals.

item.title: Unionized ProPublica staff are on strike over AI, layoffs, and wages
item.description: ProPublica staff are striking over issues related to AI, layoffs, and wages, highlighting tensions in the media industry regarding the impact of technology on jobs. The strike emphasizes the need for fair labor practices in newsrooms.

item.title: Microsoft hints at bit bunkers for war zones
item.description: Microsoft is reconsidering its datacenter designs in conflict zones, following attacks on data centers in the Middle East. This shift could enhance the resilience of tech infrastructure in unstable regions.

item.title: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
item.description: Google has launched an offline AI dictation app using Gemma AI models, aiming to compete with existing dictation tools. This innovation enhances accessibility and usability for users without internet access.

item.title: Japan relaxes privacy laws to make itself the ‘easiest country to develop AI’
item.description: Japan is easing privacy laws to become the most favorable country for AI development, eliminating the need for consent to use certain personal data. This move aims to accelerate AI innovation but raises ethical concerns.

item.title: I listened to Sony, Bose, and Apple's flagship headphones - and this pair's ahead of the pack
item.description: A comparison of flagship headphones from Apple, Bose, and Sony reveals that one model stands out in performance after real-world testing. This analysis highlights the strengths and weaknesses of each option.

item.title: I tested an $80 monitor with a 144Hz refresh rate, and it's a 'Pro' option in several ways
item.description: The MSI Pro MP243W monitor offers a 144Hz refresh rate at an affordable price, making it a strong choice for budget-conscious users. Its features cater well to home workstation needs.

item.title: Pebblebee Halo vs. AirTag: One of these trackers has a 130dB siren and strobe light
item.description: The Pebblebee Halo tracker distinguishes itself with a 130dB siren and strobe light, enhancing personal safety beyond typical tracking devices. This makes it a unique option for security-conscious consumers.

item.title: Why the 'Subwoofer Crawl' is the only way I found the bass sweet spot in my living room
item.description: The 'Subwoofer Crawl' technique helps users find the best bass placement in non-square rooms, optimizing home theater sound. This practical advice is essential for effective audio setup.

item.title: Manage AI costs with Amazon Bedrock Projects
item.description: Amazon Bedrock Projects allows users to manage AI inference costs by linking them to specific workloads, providing insights through AWS tools. This feature aids in cost analysis and project management.

item.title: Outlook: Copilot Search Suggestions for Outlook
item.description: Outlook's new Copilot feature offers search suggestions to streamline inbox management and improve productivity. This integration aims to enhance user efficiency in handling emails and calendars.

item.title: Microsoft Viva: New Overview experience and agent categories in Agent Dashboard
item.description: Microsoft Viva introduces an Overview page in its Agent Dashboard, providing insights into agent usage and adoption within organizations. This feature aims to enhance visibility and understanding of AI agents.

item.title: Microsoft Viva: Unlocking Deeper Copilot Insights with Enhanced Power BI Filtering
item.description: Enhanced filtering options in Power BI reports for Microsoft Viva allow admins to customize insights based on specific organizational needs. This upgrade aims to improve data analysis capabilities.

item.title: Outlook: Data Loss Prevention (DLP) Policy Tips & Override in Outlook for Mac
item.description: Outlook for Mac now features Data Loss Prevention (DLP) policy tips to help users avoid accidental data leaks. This enhancement alerts users when sensitive content is detected.

item.title: Building real-time conversational podcasts with Amazon Nova 2 Sonic
item.description: Amazon Nova 2 Sonic enables the creation of real-time conversational podcasts with AI hosts, showcasing its streaming and audio generation capabilities. This innovation demonstrates the potential for automated content creation.

item.title: Text-to-SQL solution powered by Amazon Bedrock
item.description: Amazon Bedrock has introduced a text-to-SQL solution that converts natural language business questions into database queries, enhancing data accessibility for users.

item.title: Prioritize security alerts with runtime context from Dynatrace
item.description: Dynatrace now allows users to prioritize GitHub Advanced Security alerts based on runtime context, improving security management in Kubernetes environments.

item.title: Verifying and optimizing post-quantum cryptography at Amazon
item.description: Amazon is focusing on verifying and optimizing post-quantum cryptography through automated reasoning, balancing security with performance and maintainability.

item.title: Copilot CLI now supports BYOK and local models
item.description: GitHub Copilot CLI has added support for bringing your own key (BYOK) and running local models, providing more flexibility for developers.

item.title: Dependabot alerts are now assignable to AI agents for remediation
item.description: Dependabot now allows users to assign alerts for dependency vulnerabilities to AI agents, facilitating more complex code remediation.

item.title: Dependabot version updates now support the Nix ecosystem
item.description: Dependabot has expanded its capabilities to support the Nix ecosystem, enabling better version management for Nix flakes.

item.title: OpenAI encourages firms to trial four-day weeks to adapt to AI era
item.description: OpenAI is advocating for companies to experiment with four-day work weeks as a response to the evolving capabilities of AI technologies.

item.title: context-stats added to PyPI
item.description: The context-stats package has been added to PyPI, allowing developers to monitor their Claude Code session context in real-time.

item.title: SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic could reopen the IPO market—or drain it
item.description: Startups like SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic are influencing the IPO market, with established unicorns struggling to demonstrate AI growth potential.

item.title: a11y-expert-mcp added to PyPI
item.description: The a11y-expert-mcp package has been introduced to PyPI, aiding developers in building accessible components with guidance on WAI-ARIA patterns and WCAG standards.

item.title: Show HN: Open-Source Higgsfiled AI Alternative
item.description: An open-source alternative to Higgsfield AI has been launched, offering free AI image generation and cinema studio capabilities with over 20 models. This self-hosted solution allows for customization without subscription fees.

item.title: memchinesepalace added to PyPI
item.description: A new package called memchinesepalace has been added to PyPI, focusing on using Classical Chinese as a memory compression core for language models. This innovative approach aims to enhance memory efficiency in AI applications.

item.title: Anthropic Google Broadcom TPU deal 3.5 gigawatts and $30 billion revenue
item.description: Anthropic has secured a deal with Broadcom for 3.5 gigawatts of Google TPU capacity, significantly boosting its AI infrastructure. This partnership is expected to enhance Anthropic's capabilities as its annual revenue run rate surpasses $30 billion.

item.title: The Morning After: Apple’s foldable iPhone may be delayed
item.description: Apple's release of its foldable iPhone may be delayed due to unexpected issues encountered during early production tests. Reports suggest that these complications could push back the launch timeline.

item.title: atompack-db added to PyPI
item.description: The atompack-db has been added to PyPI, providing fast and compressed storage solutions for atomic structures and their properties. This tool aims to enhance data management in scientific research.

item.title: A wild, wide foldable iPhone dummy emerges amid rumors of a delay
item.description: Photos of a dummy unit of Apple's wide foldable iPhone have surfaced, amid rumors of delays in its release. The images also include models of the upcoming iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max.

item.title: GPUBreach exploit uses GPU memory bit-flips to achieve full system takeover
item.description: The GPUBreach exploit has been revealed, utilizing GPU memory bit-flips to escalate privileges and potentially take full control of systems. This research highlights vulnerabilities in GPU memory that could lead to severe security breaches.

item.title: Broadcom to supply Anthropic with 3.5 gigawatts of Google TPU capacity from 2027 — Claude pioneer says its annual revenue run rate has passed $30 billion
item.description: Broadcom's agreement to supply Anthropic with 3.5 gigawatts of Google TPU capacity starting in 2027 is a significant development in AI infrastructure. This deal supports Anthropic's growth as it approaches a $30 billion revenue run rate.

item.title: GenAI in product manufacturing cuts costs but adds risks
item.description: Generative AI is being utilized in product manufacturing to consolidate supplier data and improve processes, resulting in significant cost savings. However, it also introduces unique risks that need to be managed.

item.title: Show HN: Serif.sh – it's like ray.so for quotes (Open source)
item.description: Serif.sh, an open-source tool similar to ray.so for quotes, has been introduced. It allows users to create and share quote graphics easily.
